questions: Planning & Strategy: How do I set a SMART goal in Lofi?
answer: In Campaigns > Create Campaign, select your objective (Conversions, Traffic, Awareness) and enter a SMART goal in the ‘Describe your goal’ field—e.g., ‘Generate 50 leads at <$20 CPL this month.’ This gives Lofi’s AI a precise target to optimize toward, aligning bidding, creative suggestions, and pacing to your desired outcome.

questions: Planning & Strategy: Should I segment by funnel stage?
answer: Yes. Create separate campaigns for New Customers (e.g., lookalikes) and Website Visitors (custom audiences) under the Audience step. This tailoring lets you tune creative and budgets to cold vs. warm audiences without overcomplicating targeting with granular filters.

questions: Planning & Strategy: How should I name campaigns?
answer: Use descriptive names that include the objective and time frame, such as ‘June Sales – Retarget.’ Clear naming improves scannability in the Campaigns list and makes comparisons simpler during reporting.

questions: Planning & Strategy: How do I quickly view performance trends?
answer: On the Dashboard, use date presets like ‘Last 7 days’ or ‘Last 3 months,’ or pick a Custom Range. This lets you quickly compare before/after optimization windows and seasonal patterns.

questions: Budgeting & Allocation: What is the 70–20–10 rule?
answer: In Campaigns > Budgets, allocate 70% of spend to proven winners, 20% to promising tests, and 10% to experiments. This balances reliable ROI with ongoing innovation and reduces the risk of over-indexing on unproven ideas.

questions: Budgeting & Allocation: Should I use daily or lifetime budgets?
answer: Use daily budgets for consistent pacing across days; choose lifetime budgets to let Lofi optimize spend timing within your campaign window. Daily helps stabilize delivery, while lifetime can concentrate spend when performance is strongest.

questions: Budgeting & Allocation: When should I pause non-performers?
answer: If a campaign spends but does not convert after about one week, open the ‘…’ menu in the Campaigns list and select Pause. This halts wasted spend and frees budget for higher-ROI efforts. Always consider sample size and learning phases before pausing early.

questions: Budgeting & Allocation: How often should I reallocate budget?
answer: Review budgets monthly. Shift funds away from low-ROI campaigns to top performers based on ROAS, CPA, and growth goals. This keeps spend aligned with what’s working as seasonality and creative fatigue evolve.

questions: Audience & Targeting: Should I avoid over-specifying audiences?
answer: Yes. Start with broad segments (e.g., ‘Fitness Enthusiasts’) instead of stacking many narrow interests. Lofi’s AI refines delivery as it learns—overly narrow audiences can drive up costs and limit reach.

questions: Audience & Targeting: How do I get help with Pixel/Tag setup?
answer: If your Pixel or Tag isn’t installed, go to Settings > Ad Accounts and click Contact Marketing Manager. Proper conversion tracking ensures accurate attribution and optimization without requiring you to write code.

questions: Creative & Copy: How do I design mobile-first creatives?
answer: In Creative > Adobe Express, select vertical (9:16) for Stories and square (1:1) for feeds. Use bold text overlays, high contrast, and safe margins to maintain readability across placements.

questions: Creative & Copy: Should I front-load the CTA?
answer: Yes. Lead with the benefit and follow with the call-to-action, e.g., ‘Save 20% Now – Shop Today.’ This captures attention immediately and clarifies the next step.

questions: Creative & Copy: How does Lofi’s auto-rotation help?
answer: Upload multiple creatives per campaign and let Lofi auto-rotate. Underperformers are phased out and top performers get more delivery—no manual swaps required.

questions: Creative & Copy: What messaging works best for my industry?
answer:
Use these succinct, high-performing guidelines: 

E-commerce/Retail — Focus: product benefits, social proof, urgency. Tips: highlight unique features or best-seller status; mention limited-time offers; add social proof (e.g., ‘Over 10,000 sold!’). Example lines: ‘Your perfect everyday sneaker is here.’ ‘Limited stock—grab yours before it’s gone!’ CTAs: Shop Now, Get Offer, Learn More. 

Health & Wellness — Focus: transformation, trust, simplicity. Tips: use calming, result-driven language; address pain points; build trust with credentials/testimonials. Example lines: ‘Feel better, naturally—with personalized wellness plans.’ ‘Join 1,000+ clients transforming their health.’ CTAs: Book Now, Learn More, Contact Us. 

Education/Online Courses — Focus: value, outcomes, convenience. Tips: emphasize skills/certifications/career outcomes; highlight flexibility (‘self-paced,’ ‘no experience needed’); include success stats. Example lines: ‘Level up your career in 6 weeks.’ ‘Start learning today—no experience needed.’ CTAs: Sign Up, Learn More, Subscribe, Apply Now. 

Home Services/Local — Focus: trust, convenience, availability. Tips: emphasize local presence or same-day service; highlight ‘no-hassle’ and free quotes; showcase reviews/tenure. Example lines: ‘Fast, reliable AC repair—trusted by your neighbors.’ ‘Need help today? We’re one call away.’ CTAs: Get Quote, Contact Us, Book Now. 

Travel & Hospitality — Focus: inspiration, ease, limited-time offers. Tips: paint a vivid picture; mention limited availability or special rates; be aspirational. Example lines: ‘Escape to the coast—3 nights from $299.’ ‘Your next adventure starts here.’ CTAs: Book Now, Get Offer, Learn More. 

Tech/SaaS — Focus: problem-solving, features, ease of use. Tips: highlight time/cost savings; call out specific features and outcomes; promote demos/free trials. Example lines: ‘Manage everything in one dashboard—no coding needed.’ ‘Try it free—see results in days.’ CTAs: Sign Up, Learn More, Subscribe, Download. 

Media/Entertainment/Creators — Focus: engagement, intrigue, emotion. Tips: tease exclusive or trending content; use curiosity/cliffhangers; highlight emotional payoff. Example lines: ‘You won’t believe what happens next.’ ‘New episode just dropped—don’t miss it.’ CTAs: Watch More, Subscribe, Learn More.

questions: Campaign Creation & Launch: How should I use the four-step wizard?
answer: Click Create Campaign and complete Steps 1 (Strategy), 2 (Platforms), 3 (Creative), and 4 (Launch) in order. Use ‘Save as Draft’ anytime to pause and resume. Following the sequence ensures every required field is captured in a logical flow.

questions: Campaign Creation & Launch: Should I use Expert Options?
answer: Keep Expert Options collapsed unless you need specific advanced controls. Lofi’s defaults and AI optimizations are designed to handle most scenarios out of the box, reducing setup time and complexity.

questions: Campaign Creation & Launch: What should I review before launching?
answer: On the final screen, check the Campaign Overview sidebar for budget, schedule, and service-fee breakdown. Edit as needed to ensure accuracy before going live.

questions: Any final tips to streamline my Lofi workflow?
answer: Use these actionable tips and the industry messaging cheatsheet to accelerate creation while allowing Lofi's AI to handle optimization. Keep goals SMART, audiences appropriately broad, budgets disciplined via 70–20–10, and creatives mobile-first with strong CTAs. Review the campaign summary before launch and revisit budgets monthly to maintain momentum.

questions: What concrete steps improve ROAS fast?
answer: Run 3–5 creative variants, pause the bottom quartile weekly, and funnel budget to the top performers. Narrow broad audiences after 3–5 days if CTR is low; expand winning audiences gradually. Ensure message-match on landing pages and monitor cost per conversion against your target ROAS.

questions: How should I optimize weekly?
answer: Turn on Creative Optimization and add multiple variants. Use Audience Optimization to iterate segments. Reallocate budget in campaign-update or create a follow-up flight. Use Lucy to set weekly optimization tasks and shift budget toward higher-ROAS campaigns.

questions: How do I effectively test creative variants?
answer: Upload multiple creatives per campaign and let Lofi cycle them automatically. Lofi retires underperformers and surfaces best-performing assets—no manual swaps needed. Focus on testing different headlines, visuals, and CTAs to identify what resonates with your audience.

questions: How do I optimize audience targeting for better ROAS?
answer: Start with broader audiences and let Lofi's AI refine delivery. After 3-5 days, narrow audiences if CTR is low by adding more specific interests or demographics. Expand winning audiences gradually by increasing budget allocation. Use Lucy's Audience Optimization feature to automatically test and refine segments.

questions: How do I reallocate budget for maximum ROAS?
answer: Weekly, review campaign performance and shift budget from underperforming campaigns to top performers. Use the Dashboard to identify high-ROAS campaigns and increase their daily budgets. Pause campaigns that consistently underperform after giving them adequate time to optimize.
